 {"id":"bd-1gl","title":"E2E extension runtime (WASM+JS) with log schema + artifacts","description":"# Goal
Create an end-to-end extension runtime run (WASM + JS tiers) with deterministic artifacts and logs.

# Scope
- Execute the pinned sample set (`docs/extension-sample.json`) across both WASM and JS tiers where applicable.
- Ensure extc pipeline + compat scanner are used (no manual source edits).
- Exercise hostcalls, tool calls, event hooks, and UI hooks where supported.
- Validate both success and expected-deny cases (capability policy).

# Logging / Artifacts
- Emit JSONL logs per bd-4u9: scenario id, extension id, tier, policy mode, timings, and redaction summary.
- Capture artifacts: compat ledger, hostcall audit log, trace viewer output, stdout/stderr, and any produced files.
- Provide a deterministic artifact index and hash list.

# Tests
- E2E script (CLI or harness) with offline deterministic mode.
- Assertions for: exit status, expected outputs, and policy denials.

# Dependencies
- Runtime wiring (bd-2i5), smoke suite (bd-2ni), logging spec (bd-4u9), compat pipeline (bd-xgo).

# Acceptance Criteria
- Full pinned sample set runs with no manual edits; failures are explained with structured logs.
- Artifacts are reproducible and sufficient for diff-based debugging.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1hkm","title":"Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler (pi.tool)","description":"# Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Tool that routes pi.tool() calls from extensions to the Rust ToolRegistry.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.tool("read", {path: "foo.txt"}), the flow is:
1. JS: pi.tool() enqueues HostcallRequest with kind=Tool
2. Rust: Dispatcher drains request, extracts tool name + params
3. Rust: Looks up tool in ToolRegistry, calls execute()
4. Rust: Converts ToolOutput to HostcallOutcome
5. JS: Promise resolves with result

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.tool(name, input) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// This allows extensions to invoke any tool in the registry,
    /// including built-in tools and other extension-registered tools.
    async fn dispatch_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        // Find tool in registry
        let Some(tool) = self.tool_registry.get(name) else {
            return HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_NOT_FOUND".to_string(),
                message: format!("Tool '{}' not found in registry", name),
            };
        };
        
        // Generate unique tool_call_id for tracking
        let tool_call_id = format!("ext-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string());
        
        // Execute the tool
        match tool.execute(&tool_call_id, input, None).await {
            Ok(output) => {
                // Convert ToolOutput to JSON Value
                let result = serde_json::json!({
                    "content": output.content,
                    "details": output.details,
                });
                HostcallOutcome::Success(result)
            }
            Err(e) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

| Error Condition | Code | Behavior |
|-----------------|------|----------|
| Tool not found | TOOL_NOT_FOUND | Return error outcome |
| Tool execution fails | TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR | Return error with message |
| Invalid params | INVALID_PARAMS | Return validation error |
| Timeout | TIMEOUT | Return timeout error |

## Integration with Update Callbacks

The TypeScript reference supports streaming tool updates via the onUpdate callback. For v1, we can omit this (return final result only). Future enhancement:

```rust
// Future: Support streaming updates
let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(16);
let update_callback = Some(Box::new(move |update| {
    let _ = tx.try_send(update);
}));
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: Tool found and executed successfully
2. Unit test: Tool not found returns error
3. Unit test: Tool execution error propagates
4. Integration test: Built-in tool (read) via extension
5. Integration test: Extension-registered tool via another extension

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Blocks: End-to-end extension tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_tool() method implemented
- [ ] Tool lookup in registry works
- [ ] Successful execution returns Success outcome
- [ ] Errors return Error outcome with code + message
- [ ] Tool call IDs are unique
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.871031814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199266466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199200443Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtensionDispatcher dispatch_and_complete() for HostcallKind::Tool using ToolRegistry; added unit tests covering success + unknown tool rejection; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.885917174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:48.505416010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1i5x","title":"Implement pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() for message injection","description":"Allow extensions to inject messages into the conversation.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
// Custom message (not for LLM, optional display)
pi.sendMessage({
  customType: string,
  content: string,
  display?: boolean,
  details?: unknown
}, {
  triggerTurn?: boolean,
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp' | 'nextTurn'
});

// User message (triggers LLM turn)
pi.sendUserMessage(content, {
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp'
});
```

## Delivery Modes
- **steer**: Interrupt after current tool, skip remaining tool calls
- **followUp**: Queue after agent finishes current turn
- **nextTurn**: Default, processed as next user input

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for 'sendMessage' and 'sendUserMessage'
2. Integration with agent message queues (steering_queue, follow_up_queue)
3. Custom message type in session entries
4. Display logic in TUI for custom messages

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark', content: 'Saved\!'})
2. Hostcall creates CustomMessage entry
3. If display=true, shows in TUI
4. If triggerTurn=true, queues for processing

## Test Plan
- Unit test: sendMessage creates correct entry type
- Unit test: sendUserMessage triggers agent turn
- Integration test: steer mode interrupts current processing
- Integration test: followUp mode queues correctly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/extensions_js.rs (bridge to Rust)
- src/agent.rs (message queue integration)
- src/interactive.rs (display custom messages)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.477300174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:35.499225524Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.494460870Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:35.499164360Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ilq","title":"Implement pi.registerShortcut() for extension keyboard bindings","description":"Allow extensions to register custom keyboard shortcuts.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerShortcut(key, {
  description: string,
  handler: (ctx) => Promise<void>
});
```

Key format: 'ctrl+u', 'alt+shift+x', 'f1', etc.

## Implementation Requirements
1. ShortcutRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerShortcut'
3. Integration with keybindings system (src/keybindings.rs)
4. Validation that key isn't reserved (interrupt, clear, exit, suspend)
5. Shortcut execution via extension runtime

## Reserved Keys (cannot be overridden)
- ctrl+c (interrupt)
- ctrl+d (exit)
- ctrl+l (clear)
- ctrl+z (suspend)

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+u', {...})
2. Hostcall validates key not reserved, stores in registry
3. KeyMsg in bubbletea checked against extension shortcuts
4. If match, invoke JS handler with ExtensionContext

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerShortcut stores shortcut
- Unit test: reserved keys rejected
- Integration test: registered shortcut triggers handler

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ShortcutRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/keybindings.rs (integrate with extension shortcuts)
- src/interactive.rs (dispatch to extension handler on key)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.738747397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:32.254817020Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.745921073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:32.254783057Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1iwi","title":"Workstream: Interactive Editor UX Parity (autocomplete, multiline, bang, images)","description":"# Goal
Bring the **interactive editor experience** to parity with legacy pi-mono, focusing on:
- Autocomplete (commands, templates, skills, file references, path completion)
- Multi-line editing behavior (Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits)
- Bash shortcuts (`!` and `!!`)
- Clipboard image paste + drag/drop attachments

# Legacy Behavior (Source-of-Truth)
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Editor”:
- File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files
- Path completion: Tab to complete paths
- Multi-line: Shift+Enter (Ctrl+Enter on Windows Terminal)
- Images: Ctrl+V to paste, or drag onto terminal
- Bash commands: `!command` runs and sends output to LLM, `!!command` runs without sending

Legacy implementation details:
- Autocomplete is provided by `CombinedAutocompleteProvider` (slash commands, prompt templates, extension commands, skill commands, file path completion using `fd`).
- Image paste writes clipboard image to a temp file and inserts the file path into the editor.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/interactive.rs` reserves Tab for future autocomplete but has no autocomplete UI.
- No `@` file fuzzy search.
- No Shift+Enter newline semantics (current behavior differs).
- No `!` / `!!` bash shortcuts.
- No clipboard image paste handler.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Autocomplete engine
- Provide suggestions for:
  - Slash commands (built-in)
  - Prompt templates (`/<template>`)
  - Skill commands (`/skill:name`) when enabled
  - File references after typing `@` (fuzzy search project files)
  - Path completion when cursor is inside a path token

## 2) Autocomplete UI
- Render a dropdown/list with:
  - configurable max visible items (`autocomplete_max_visible`)
  - keyboard navigation
  - selection/accept behavior

## 3) Multi-line input semantics
- Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits.
- Windows Terminal: Ctrl+Enter inserts newline.

## 4) Bash shortcuts
- `!cmd`: run and include output in LLM context.
- `!!cmd`: run but do not include output in LLM context.

## 5) Clipboard image paste and drag/drop
- Ctrl+V attempts to read clipboard image:
  - if an image exists, write it to a temp file and insert the file path at cursor.
  - do nothing (silent) if clipboard has no image or access denied.
- Drag/drop: terminals usually paste a file path; ensure we handle file paths cleanly.

# Testing
- Unit tests for autocomplete matching and insertion.
- State tests for key interactions (Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+V).
- Integration tests for `!` / `!!` behavior (using deterministic command runner where possible).

# Dependencies
- Keybinding routing (`bd-3ip`) for Tab / Shift+Enter / Ctrl+V behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits
[ ] ! and !! bash shortcuts implemented
[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)

 {"id":"bd-30hr","title":"Proptest: truncation invariants (lines/bytes/head-tail)","description":"## Goal
Add property-based tests for truncation logic to ensure invariants hold across random inputs.

## Targets
- Shared truncation utilities used by tools (`read`, `bash`, `grep`, `find`, `ls`).

## Invariants to encode
- Output never exceeds configured byte/line budgets.
- Head/tail truncation markers appear when (and only when) truncation occurs.
- Truncation is stable: applying truncation twice yields the same output.
- UTF-8 validity is preserved (no broken codepoints).
- Line numbering (where applicable) remains consistent and monotonic.

## Acceptance
- proptest suite runs quickly in CI (< ~1s per test module).
- Any failing minimal cases are promoted into a deterministic unit test.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-30j8","title":"Task: Implement Events Hostcall Handler (pi.events)","description":"# Task: Implement Events Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Events that manages extension event subscriptions and provides event-related functionality.

## Background

The pi.events() hostcall handles event-related operations:
- Subscribe to events
- Unsubscribe from events
- Get list of subscribed events
- Emit custom events

Note: This is different from event DISPATCH (agent calling into extensions). This is for extension-initiated event operations.

## Current State

The event hook registration system exists in extensions_js.rs (__pi_register_hook), but it's JS-side only. The Events hostcall provides a Rust-side interface for event operations that extensions might need.

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.events(op, args) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// Provides event-related operations for extensions:
    /// - list: Get list of registered event hooks
    /// - emit: Emit a custom event to other extensions
    async fn dispatch_events(
        &self,
        op: &str,
        args: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        match op {
            "list" => {
                // Return list of events this extension is subscribed to
                let events = self.runtime.list_registered_events();
                HostcallOutcome::Success(serde_json::json!({
                    "events": events,
                }))
            }
            
            "emit" => {
                // Emit a custom event to other extensions
                let event_name = args.get("event")
                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                    .ok_or("Missing event name")?;
                
                let event_data = args.get("data")
                    .cloned()
                    .unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Null);
                
                // Dispatch to all registered handlers for this event
                match self.runtime.dispatch_custom_event(event_name, event_data).await {
                    Ok(results) => {
                        HostcallOutcome::Success(serde_json::json!({
                            "dispatched": true,
                            "handlerCount": results.len(),
                        }))
                    }
                    Err(e) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                        code: "EVENT_ERROR".to_string(),
                        message: e.to_string(),
                    },
                }
            }
            
            _ => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "UNKNOWN_OPERATION".to_string(),
                message: format!("Unknown events operation: {}", op),
            },
        }
    }
}
```

## Event System Architecture

```
Extension A                    Extension B
     │                              │
     │ pi.events.emit("custom")     │
     ▼                              │
HostcallKind::Events ──────────────►│
     │                              │
     │ dispatch_custom_event()      │
     │                              ▼
     │                    pi.on("custom", handler)
     │                              │
     └──────────────────────────────┘
```

## Custom Event Use Cases

- Extension coordination (one extension notifying others)
- Plugin system where extensions can extend each other
- Debugging/tracing hooks

## Testing

1. Unit test: list returns subscribed events
2. Unit test: emit dispatches to handlers
3. Unit test: Unknown operation returns error
4. Integration test: Cross-extension event communication

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Related: Event hook dispatch feature (separate task)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_events() method implemented
- [ ] list operation returns event list
- [ ] emit operation dispatches custom events
- [ ] Unknown operations return error

-{"id":"bd-30u","title":"Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios","description":"# Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios

## Goal
Create recorded cassettes for all Anthropic provider test scenarios.

## Scenarios to Record

### Happy Path Scenarios
1. **Simple text response** - Single text block, stop reason "stop"
2. **Multi-paragraph response** - Multiple text deltas
3. **Extended thinking** - thinking_start/delta/end + text response
4. **Tool call single** - Single tool_call in response
5. **Tool call multiple** - Multiple tool_calls in single response
6. **Tool result processing** - tool_result message handling

### Error Scenarios
7. **Rate limit (429)** - With retry-after header
8. **Auth failure (401)** - Invalid API key
9. **Forbidden (403)** - Resource access denied
10. **Bad request (400)** - Malformed request
11. **Server error (500)** - Internal server error
12. **Overloaded (529)** - Anthropic overloaded

### Edge Cases
13. **Empty response** - No content blocks
14. **Very long response** - Hits max_tokens
15. **Unicode content** - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
16. **Large tool call args** - Big JSON in arguments
17. **Stream interruption** - Partial response simulation

### Extended Thinking Variants
18. **Thinking only** - No final text, just thinking
19. **Thinking with tool calls** - Thinking then tool use
20. **Thinking budget exceeded** - Hits thinking token limit

## Recording Process
```bash
# Set API key (not committed)
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Record mode
VCR_MODE=record cargo test provider_streaming::anthropic

# Verify cassettes created
ls tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_*.json
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) during recording runs.
- Log cassette name/path, model, prompt summary hash, and redaction summary.
- Capture command line + env (redacted) used to record each cassette.

## Determinism + Redaction
- Keep prompts/tool schemas identical across recordings; store prompt hashes in a manifest.
- Ensure API keys and any sensitive fields are redacted.
- Record with fixed model versions + params (temperature, max_tokens, thinking level).

## Files Created
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_simple_text.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_extended_thinking.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_tool_call.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_rate_limit.json
- ... (20 cassettes total)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_manifest.json (scenario → prompt hash + params)

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure must exist)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20 cassettes recorded
- [ ] All scenarios covered
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Cassettes valid JSON
- [ ] Manifest includes prompt hashes + params
- [ ] Playback matches recording

 {"id":"bd-30zd","title":"Docs: README - correct asupersync vs tokio migration status","description":"## Goal
Update `README.md` so it accurately reflects the current implementation state.

## Background
`README.md` currently states that tokio “handles the main runtime” and that the asupersync migration is ongoing. In the current codebase, `Cargo.toml` already uses `asupersync` as the async runtime and there are no tokio/reqwest references in `src/`.

## Scope
- Remove/replace statements implying tokio is still in use.
- Ensure the “Foundation Libraries / asupersync” section describes the *current* architecture (runtime + HTTP/TLS + cancellation model).
- Update any examples or wording that imply reqwest/tokio.
- Keep extension-related sections intact (we are not changing extension plans here).

## Acceptance
- README contains no claims that contradict `Cargo.toml` + `src/main.rs`.
- README’s architecture description matches how the binary actually runs today.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-30zg","title":"Epic: Extensions Runtime Integration (100% Parity)","description":"# Epic: Extensions Runtime Integration

## Executive Summary

This epic tracks all work required to achieve 100% feature parity for the Pi Agent extensions runtime. The Rust port has ~70-80% of the extensions infrastructure implemented (QuickJS runtime, Promise bridge, capability policy, protocol validation) but is missing the critical agent integration layer that wires everything together.

## Background & Context

The TypeScript Pi Agent supports a powerful extensions system that allows:
- Custom tool registration (pi.registerTool)
- Slash command registration (pi.registerCommand)
- Event hook interception (pi.on for lifecycle events)
- UI interactions (dialogs, notifications, status updates)
- Session access (messages, state, entries)

The Rust port has implemented the JavaScript runtime (PiJS using QuickJS) with:
- Full Promise bridge architecture (native Rust → JS Promise → completion)
- All 6 hostcall types defined (tool, exec, http, session, ui, events)
- Deterministic event loop with timer management
- TypeScript transpilation via SWC
- Virtual module shims for Node.js compatibility
- Capability manifest and policy enforcement

## What's Missing (The Integration Layer)

The gap is the "glue" between the agent loop and the extension runtime:

1. **Hostcall Dispatcher**: Extensions call pi.tool(), pi.http(), etc. These enqueue HostcallRequest objects, but nothing processes them.

2. **Tool Execution Bridge**: Extensions can register tools, but the agent can't execute them.

3. **Event Hook Dispatch**: Extensions register hooks via pi.on(), but the agent doesn't fire events at lifecycle points.

4. **Slash Command Handler**: Extensions register commands, but /command invocations don't reach the JS runtime.

5. **Session Context Binding**: The ExtensionSession trait is defined but not bound to actual session state.

6. **UI Marshaling**: Extension UI requests need to flow to TUI or RPC layer.

## Success Criteria

- All extension fixture tests pass
- Extensions can register and execute tools
- Extensions can intercept and modify tool results via events
- Slash commands registered by extensions are executable
- Event hooks fire at all documented lifecycle points
- UI dialogs work in both TUI and RPC modes
- Benchmark targets met for hostcall dispatch (<100µs)

## References

- src/extensions.rs (protocol, policy, connectors)
- src/extensions_js.rs (QuickJS runtime, Promise bridge)
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md Section 12 (Extensions API spec)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:51:34.985921435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:07:30.119745520Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-318h","title":"Task: Implement tool_result event dispatch (modifying)","description":"# Task: Implement tool_result Event Dispatch (Modifying)

## Objective

Dispatch the tool_result event after tool execution, allowing extensions to modify or observe results.

## Background

The tool_result event enables:
- Logging: Record all tool outputs
- Transformation: Modify content/details before returning to model
- Error enrichment: Add context to errors

## TypeScript Reference (wrapper.ts:74-90)

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers("tool_result")) {
    const resultResult = await runner.emit({
        type: "tool_result",
        toolName: tool.name,
        toolCallId,
        input: params,
        content: result.content,
        details: result.details,
        isError: false,
    });

    if (resultResult) {
        return {
            content: resultResult.content ?? result.content,
            details: (resultResult.details ?? result.details) as T,
        };
    }
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn execute_tool_with_events(
        &self,
        tool_call: &ToolCall,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        // [tool_call event dispatch - see previous task]
        
        // Execute tool
        let result = match self.execute_tool_raw(tool_call).await {
            Ok(output) => output,
            Err(e) => {
                // Dispatch tool_result for errors too
                if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
                    if dispatcher.has_handlers("tool_result") {
                        let event = ExtensionEvent::ToolResult {
                            tool_name: tool_call.name.clone(),
                            tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
                            input: tool_call.arguments.clone(),
                            content: vec![ContentBlock::Text(TextContent {
                                text: e.to_string(),
                                text_signature: None,
                            })],
                            details: None,
                            is_error: true,
                        };
                        let _ = dispatcher.dispatch::<ToolResultEventResult>(event).await;
                    }
                }
                return Err(e);
            }
        };
        
        // Dispatch tool_result for success
        let final_result = if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("tool_result") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::ToolResult {
                    tool_name: tool_call.name.clone(),
                    tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
                    input: tool_call.arguments.clone(),
                    content: result.content.clone(),
                    details: result.details.clone(),
                    is_error: false,
                };
                
                if let Some(modification) = dispatcher.dispatch::<ToolResultEventResult>(event).await? {
                    ToolOutput {
                        content: modification.content.unwrap_or(result.content),
                        details: modification.details.or(result.details),
                    }
                } else {
                    result
                }
            } else {
                result
            }
        } else {
            result
        };
        
        Ok(final_result)
    }
}
```

## Event Data Format

```json
{
    "type": "tool_result",
    "toolName": "read",
    "toolCallId": "call-abc123",
    "input": { "path": "secret.txt" },
    "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "file contents" }],
    "details": { "lines_read": 10 },
    "isError": false
}
```

## Handler Example (Extension)

```javascript
ctx.on("tool_result", async (event) => {
    if (event.toolName === "read") {
        // Redact sensitive content
        const redacted = event.content.map(c => ({
            ...c,
            text: c.text.replace(/password=.*/gi, "password=[REDACTED]")
        }));
        return { content: redacted };
    }
    return null; // No modification
});
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No handlers → returns original result
2. Unit test: Handler returns null → returns original
3. Unit test: Handler modifies content → returns modified
4. Unit test: Handler modifies details → returns modified
5. Unit test: Error results also dispatched
6. Integration test: Extension redacts sensitive data

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Depends on: bd-35hz (tool_call dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] tool_result event dispatched after execution
- [ ] Modifications applied to result
- [ ] Null returns preserve original
- [ ] Error results also trigger event
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:11.841927179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:04:03.793714135Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:04:03.793644967Z","close_reason":"tool_result hook dispatch (modifying) wired + tested","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-318h","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:11.851435357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-318h","depends_on_id":"bd-35hz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:30.661782494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
